questions.question: Which European companies competed with the English East India Company in India?
questions.answer: The Portuguese, Dutch and French trading companies competed with the English East India Company.

questions.question: What privileges did the Mughal emperor grant to the East India Company?
questions.answer: The Mughal emperor granted the Company trading rights and permission to set up factories.

questions.question: Why did the East India Company want to control Bengal?
questions.answer: Bengal was rich in agricultural production and trade resources.

questions.question: What was the main cause of the conflict between Siraj-ud-Daulah and the Company?
questions.answer: The Company misused its trading privileges and built fortifications without permission.

questions.question: Who were the allies of the East India Company in the Battle of Plassey?
questions.answer: Mir Jafar and other dissatisfied nobles supported the Company.

questions.question: Which three rulers fought against the Company in the Battle of Buxar?
questions.answer: Mir Qasim, Shuja-ud-Daulah and Shah Alam II fought against the Company.

questions.question: Why was the Battle of Buxar more significant than the Battle of Plassey?
questions.answer: It confirmed the Company's political and military dominance in northern India.

questions.question: What administrative power did the Company gain through the Diwani?
questions.answer: The power to collect revenue and manage civil administration.

questions.question: Why did revenue collection become crucial for the Company's expansion?
questions.answer: Revenue financed the Company's army and administrative system.

questions.question: Which governor-general introduced the Subsidiary Alliance?
questions.answer: Lord Wellesley introduced the Subsidiary Alliance.

questions.question: What condition did rulers accept under the Subsidiary Alliance?
questions.answer: They had to keep British troops and pay for their maintenance.

questions.question: Why did the Subsidiary Alliance reduce the power of Indian rulers?
questions.answer: Because they lost control over their military and foreign policy.

questions.question: Which governor-general introduced the Doctrine of Lapse?
questions.answer: Lord Dalhousie introduced the Doctrine of Lapse.

questions.question: Why did the Doctrine of Lapse cause resentment among Indian rulers?
questions.answer: Because it allowed the British to annex states without heirs.

questions.question: Which Indian ruler tried to modernize the army and administration in Mysore?
questions.answer: Tipu Sultan attempted to modernize the army and administration.

questions.question: Why did the British consider Mysore strategically important?
questions.answer: Because of its location and strong resistance to British expansion.

questions.question: What internal weakness of the Marathas helped the British defeat them?
questions.answer: The Marathas were divided among several chiefs.

questions.question: Which region became the financial base of the East India Company after 1765?
questions.answer: Bengal became the financial base of the Company.

questions.question: Why did the British annex Awadh in 1856?
questions.answer: They accused the Nawab of misgovernance and annexed the region.

questions.question: How did military strength contribute to British expansion?
questions.answer: A strong army allowed the British to defeat powerful Indian states.

questions.question: What role did diplomacy play in the Company's success?
questions.answer: Diplomacy helped the Company form alliances and weaken opponents.

questions.question: Why were Indian rulers unable to unite against the British?
questions.answer: They had political rivalries and lacked unity.

questions.question: What was the relationship between trade and territorial expansion for the Company?
questions.answer: Trade profits financed military expansion and territorial control.

questions.question: What long-term change did the Company's expansion bring to India?
questions.answer: It led to the establishment of British colonial rule.

questions.question: How did the Company's political power grow after gaining Diwani rights?
questions.answer: Control over revenue strengthened administration and military expansion.
